changes.motivation: NATS pods had label `panfactum.com/pull-through-cache-enabled: false`, causing the Kyverno ClusterPolicy to skip image URL rewriting. The production config set `pull_through_cache_enabled = true` on the `demo_cicd` module, but that value was never forwarded down the chain to `kube_argo_event_bus`, leaving its default of `false` in effect.
changes.summary: Added `pull_through_cache_enabled = var.pull_through_cache_enabled` to the `kube_argo_event_bus` module call in `infrastructure/demo_cicd/event_bus.tf` so the top-level setting propagates to the NATS pods.

problems: The agent initially misdiagnosed the root cause as a bad default value in `kube_argo_event_bus/vars.tf` rather than a missing variable forwarding in `event_bus.tf`. The actual issue was that `event_bus.tf` simply never passed `pull_through_cache_enabled` to the module, despite the parent module receiving it correctly. Better naming or a lint check for unforwarded pass-through variables would have made this more obvious.

The `kube_argo_event_bus` module's `pull_through_cache_enabled` variable defaulted to `false` while analogous modules like `kube_nats` default to `true`. This inconsistency obscured the root cause and made diagnosis harder — a uniform default of `true` across all modules would prevent silent failures when callers omit the variable.
